title: AI labs push DNA screening as safety shifts to supply chains - TCR 06/04/26
description:
OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Microsoft AI, and Meta backed 4-part synthetic DNA order screening, Google funded a 3-year 100-megawatt PJM virtual power plant, and the Allen Institute launched a $400 million Brain Health accelerator.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-4-2026/

0:00 AI labs push DNA screening
1:19 Open model powers worm test
6:29 Agents enter financial infrastructure
11:23 Google funds virtual power plant
15:59 Allen Institute targets brain repair
20:44 Close

This episode covers synthetic DNA screening and AI biosecurity, the open-model self-spreading worm, Microsoft Project Solara and Meta Business Agent, Morgan Stanley agent access through MCP, Google’s Voltus virtual power plant, and Allen Institute gene therapy work for brain disease.

The spine of the episode is safety moving into distinct gates: DNA order forms, patch pipelines, credential custody, permission records, and audit logs. Rival AI labs asked lawmakers to require screening for synthetic nucleic acid orders, while an open-source model showed how dangerous cyber automation can spread through known vulnerabilities below the frontier tier. Microsoft, Meta, and Morgan Stanley pushed agent-first interfaces into devices, business messaging, and regulated financial infrastructure, making custody and revocation central design problems. Google’s 100-megawatt PJM virtual power plant and Massachusetts vehicle-to-grid work show compute demand pulling new flexibility from existing assets. The Allen Institute’s $400 million Brain Health accelerator shows medicine moving from brain maps toward cell-level genetic intervention.
